A proposed News Bargaining Incentive would tax the three big tech companies 2.25% on ​their local revenues unless they struck agreements, with the proceeds to be directed to news companies to boost Australian journalism.  "People are increasingly getting their news directly from Facebook, from TikTok and from Google, and we believe it's only fair that large digital platforms contribute ‌to the hard work of journalism ⁠that enriches their feeds and that drives their revenue," Communications Minister Anika Wells told a news conference.
